div.h1: Activation Function Impact on Quantization Distribution
div.p: How ReLU, GELU, and SiLU shape activation statistics and calibration strategy
Color Legend: 🔴 Red = Asymmetric / ReLU Path (Positive only) 🔵 Blue = Symmetric / GELU Path 🟢 Green = Symmetric / SiLU Path / Optimal Path

g.text: Activation | Symmetry | Recommended Mode | Zero-Point | Correction
ReLU | ASYMMETRIC | Asymmetric INT8/UINT8 | zp != 0 | Required
GELU | SYMMETRIC | Symmetric INT8 | zp = 0 | None
SiLU | SYMMETRIC | Symmetric INT8 | zp = 0 | None
Symmetric mode preferred for hardware acceleration (TensorCores/DSP)

ul.li: If ReLU: Collect max only -> Set min=0 -> Map to [0, 255].
If GELU/SiLU: Collect abs_max -> Set range = [-abs_max, abs_max].

ol.li: ReLU (Asymmetric): Forces the use of the (q - zp) term in MAC operations. This adds 1 extra addition per product, increasing latency.
GELU/SiLU (Symmetric): Zero-point is 0. MAC becomes q_w * q_x, enabling "Fast Path" SIMD kernels.
Calibration Data: ReLU requires asymmetric min/max tracking (usually [0, max]). GELU/SiLU require symmetric tracking.
